title: Brown bread
excerpt: What's nicer than a slice of warm bread with a thick layer of butter! The batter gives you 2 loaves in the smaller pans. Always good to spoil someone with fresh bread. Ingredients 1 kg brown bread flour 2 x packets 10 g instant yeast 10 ml salt 60 ml sugar 600 …
html:
What's nicer than a slice of warm bread with a thick layer of butter! The batter gives you 2 loaves in the smaller pans. Always good to spoil someone with fresh bread.

Ingredients

  • 1 kg brown bread flour
  • 2 x packets 10 g instant yeast
  • 10 ml salt
  • 60 ml sugar
  • 600 ml lukewarm water

Method

  1. Mix 200 ml of the lukewarm water, sugar and instant yeast in a cup. Let it stand for 10 minutes until it goes nice and foamy.
  2. In your mixing bowl pour your flour and salt.
  3. Now add the water with yeast to the flour and start working it in with a fork or your hands.
  4. Now slowly add the rest of the water and work it until you get a firm dough.
  5. Sprinkle flour on your kitchen counter and knead the dough for at least 10 minutes on your surface.
  6. The dough will come away from your hands and feel elastic. Rub a little oil in your mixing bowl and put the dough in it. Rub a little oil on your dough. Cover it with a tea towel and let it rise until double its size. ( If I'm in a hurry, or it's cold, I set my oven to 35 C and put it in my oven covered with a cloth.)
  7. Grease 2 bread pans and divide the dough between the pans. Cover again and let it rise again until double the size.
  8. Bake it at 180C for 60 minutes. Tap on the bread and if it sounds hollow, then it's done.
